title: Claytonia litoralis
intro: Claytonia litoralis, also known as beach springbeauty, is a low-growing perennial plant that belongs to the family Montiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. Claytonia litoralis is rich in bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in supplements and capsules, to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Claytonia litoralis is a species of flowering plant in the family Montiaceae. It is native to the Pacific coast of North America. The genus Claytonia is part of the order Caryophyllales. Claytonia litoralis is further classified as a species of the genus Claytonia. It is a coastal spring beauty that blooms in early spring with white or pink flowers.
